narrative_summary:
The BRCA2 missense variant p.Arg2888Pro (c.8663G>C) alters a conserved residue in the DNA‑damage repair protein BRCA2, a gene in which pathogenic variants confer hereditary breast and ovarian cancer risk. The variant is listed in the literature but no explicit clinical classification has been reported.

A multiplexed next‑generation sequencing functional assay performed in mouse embryonic stem cells measured a functional score for the R2888P substitution, but the authors did not provide an interpretation of that score with respect to pathogenicity; the data are presented without a claim of deleterious or neutral effect [PMC10694496].

Because no clinical case data, population frequency information, or computational predictions are supplied, and the functional assay does not resolve the variant’s impact, the current evidence is insufficient to assign a definitive pathogenic or benign status. At present the variant remains of uncertain significance pending further functional or clinical investigation.

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study presents a multiplexed NGS‑based mouse ESC functional assay and includes functional score data for the BRCA2 R2888P variant, but does not explicitly interpret its pathogenicity.
article_mentions.title: Sequencing-based functional assays for classification of BRCA2 variants in mouse ESCs
